note: Sloping pointed majuscule | Contrast between thick and thin strokes; presence of serifs at the ends of thin strokes; the vertical strokes of 'rho', 'ypsilon', 'phi' end with a curve to the left; 'beta' is maximized and has round curves; the oblique strokes of kappa are detached from the vertical stroke; the central strokes of mu are merged into a curve; omega has rounded curves; angle of inclination ca. 118°

bib.citation: Assemani, Joseph S., ed. Sancti Patris Nostri Ephraem Syri opera omnia quae exstant graece, syriace, latine in sex tomos distributa. Vol. I, Graece et latine. Romae, 1732
note: The text of this section of the palimpsest presents many differences from the Assemani edition; references to the Assemani edition do not always have a precise equivalent

bib.citation: Hjelt, Arthur, ed. Syrus sinaiticus. Helsingfors, 1930
Voicu, Sever J. Frammenti di un palinsesto greco di Efrem (Sinaiticus Syr. 30, f. 171ff.). Scriptorium 38 (1984)

program.label: Sinai Palimpsests Project
program.description: Imaged and studied as part of the Sinai Palimpsests Project (2011–2016), a collaboration between St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, the Early Manuscripts Electronic Library, and UCLA Library, funded by the Arcadia Fund and the Ahmanson Foundation. The project used multispectral imaging and scholarly analysis to recover and document erased and obscured texts in palimpsest manuscripts from the monastery’s collection.
